rulings.Case Title: RONALD RODRIGUEZ VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To Ccp 1094.5
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 30, 2010, line 9, PETITIONER RONALD RODRIGUEZ Motion For Writ Of Mandamus Pursuant To CCP 1094.5. DENIED. THE COURT USES ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT IN REVIEWING THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE ENTIRE RECORD AND FINDS THAT BASED ON ITS INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT, THE DECISION OF THE HEARING OFFICER IS SUPPORTED BY THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. NO MEDICAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS PETITIONER'S CLAIM THAT HE WAS UNABLE TO PERFORM HIS DUTIES AS AN ACTING BATTALION CHIEF. DR. MANACE FOUND THAT PETITIONER EXPERIENCED NO SIGNIFICANT IMPAIRMENT IN HIS JOB PERFORMANCE AS A FIREFIGHTER AS A RESULT OF HIS HEARING LOSS. (AR 63) HE FURTHER NOTED THAT "PROVIDED [PETITIONER] MAKES REGULAR USE OF APPROVED EAR PROTECTION WHENEVER EXPOSED TO HIGH-INTENSITY OCCUPATIONAL NOISE, THERE ARE NO RESTRICTIONS TO HIS EMPLOYABILITY. WERE HE NOT RETIRED, BASED ON THE ADEQUATE AUDITORY RESERVE, [PETITIONER] COULD RESUME HIS PRIOR JOB CLASSIFICATION AS A FIREFIGHTER WITHOUT RESTRICTION." (AR 67) DR. SCHINDLER NOTED A NONRATABLE HEARING LOSS AT THE TIME OF PETITIONER'S RETIREMENT. (AR 57.) THE RECORD SUPPORTS A FINDING OF AN INDUSTRIAL - CAUSED HEARING LOSS, BUT NOTHING IN THE RECORD SUPPORTS PETITIONER'S CONTENTION THAT HE WAS DISABLED FROM PERFORMING HIS USUAL DUTIES UNTIL HE CHOSE TO RETIRE. HE ACTIVELY PERFORMED HIS DUTIES AS AN ACTING BATTALION CHIEF THROUGH THE DATE OF HIS RETIREMENT, AND NO PHYSICIAN CONSIDERED HIS HEARING LOSS TO HAVE RENDERED HIM UNABLE TO PERFORM THOSE DUTIES. =(302/CWW)
